rulings.Case Title: JOHN GIDDING et al VS. IVAN KAYE et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Amended Notice Of Motion And Motion To Hold Judgment Debtor In Contempt For Failure To Comply With Court'S Order Compelling Responses To Discovery & For Issuance Of Body Attachment
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, October 10, 2013, Line 1: Defendant GLENDONBROOK WINES PTY LTD'S Amended Notice Of Motion And Motion To Hold Judgment Debtor In Contempt For Failure To Comply With Court's Order Compelling Responses To Discovery & For Issuance Of Body Attachment Pro Tem Judge Katherine Huibonhoa, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem has not issued a tentative ruling on this motion. Each party is required to bring a proposed order to the hearing. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: CREDITORS TRADE ASSOCIATION INC.,DBA GREAT VS. BLUE FLOWER LLC A CALIFORNIA LIMITED LIABILITY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Dismiss Pursuant To Ccp Sec 583.250(A) For Failure To Serve Summons Within Three Years Of Commencement Of The Action; Mtn To Quash Service And Vacate Default Judgment
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Thursday, October 10, 2013, Line 2, DEFENDANT MATTHEW MERMOD'S Notice Of Motion And Motion To Dismiss Pursuant To CCP Sec 583.250(A) For Failure To Serve Summons Within Three Years Of Commencement Of The Action; Motion To Quash Service And Vacate Default Judgment Motion to Dismiss pursuant to CCP § 583.250(A) is denied. Estoppel applies. A person may not lull another into a false sense of security by conduct causing the latter to forebear to do something which he otherwise would have done and then take advantage of the inaction caused by his own conduct. (Biss v. Bohr (1995) 40 Cal.App.4th 1246, 1252.) Defendant entered into discussions with Plaintiff to settle the debt, which would have caused Plaintiff to reasonably believe that Defendant was properly served. Motion to Vacate Default is denied. A motion for relief from judgment valid on its face but void for improper service is governed by analogy to CCP §473.5, and therefore, relief must be sought no later than 2 years after entry of the default judgment. (See Rogers v. Silverman (1989) 216 Cal.App.3d 1114, 1121-1122.) Motion to Quash Service of Summons is denied. A default judgment must first be set aside before a motion to quash may be ruled on. As discussed above, the default judgment stands and therefore a motion to quash would be procedurally improper. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/LCN)

rulings.Case Title: UN PLAZA LLC, A CALIFORNIA LIMITED LIABILITY VS. UN PLAZA ASSOCIATES, LP A CALIFORNIA LIMITED et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Thursday, October 10, 2013, Line 9, CROSS DEFENDANTS LIBERTY ASSET MANAGEMENT CORP., LUCY GAO, BENNY KO, RED DOT INVESTMENTS, INC, TED HSU'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT Cross-Defendants Liberty Asset Management Corp., Lucy Gao, and Benny Ko's demurrer to Cross-Complainant UN Plaza Associates, L.P.'s Cross-Complaint is overruled as to the first, second, and fourth causes of action, and sustained with leave to amend as to the third cause of action. Cross-Defendants' request for judicial notice is granted. The demurrer to the first cause of action is overruled as Cross-Complainant has alleged sufficient facts, with particularity, to establish a claim for intentional misrepresentation. (Cross-Complaint, 13, 17-26.) Cross-Complainant alleges that Cross-Defendants, through their agent, misrepresented the existing facts that the potential purchasers were all cash buyers with a UBS account containing all of the cash to buy the property. (Cross Complaint, 13.) The demurrer to the second cause of action for negligent misrepresentation is overruled as Cross-Complainant has alleged sufficient facts, with particularity, that Cross-Defendants, through their agent, negligently misrepresented to Cross-Complainant that the potential purchaser was an all cash buyer that had no need for a pre-closing loan or additional equity. (Cross-Complaint, 28.) The demurrer to the fourth cause of action for alter ego is overruled as Cross-Complainant has alleged sufficient facts that a unity of interest existed between Cross-Defendants and that adherence to the separate existed would sanction fraud and promote injustice. (Cross-Complaint, 50-51.) The demurrer to the third cause of action is sustained with leave to amend. Cross-Complainant is given 10 days leave, if it can do in good faith, to plead additional facts that Cross-Defendants Hsu and Red Dot intentionally induced Cross-Complainants to rely on their misrepresentation(s). Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/LCN)

rulings.Case Title: EDWARD STOLZ et al VS. HANS PETERSON FLEISCHNER et al*****CASE TRANSFERRED TO RIVERSIDE COUNTY*****
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For An Order Transferring This Action The Proper Court; Notice Of Request For Sancitons In The Form Of Reasonable Expenses And Attorney'S Fees Incurred In Making This Motion To Transfer
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Thursday, October 10, 2013, Line 10, DEFENDANT HANS FLEISCHNER'S Notice Of Motion For An Order Transferring This Action The Proper Court; Notice Of Request For Sancitons In The Form Of Reasonable Expenses And Attorney's Fees Incurred In Making This Motion To Transfer Granted. CCP §395(a) applies because the causes of action arise from a contractual relationship between the parties. Under CCP §395(a), venue is proper in the county: (1) where the obligation is to be performed, (2) where the contract was entered into, and (3) where the defendant resides. CCP §395(a) further provides that the county where the obligation is incurred is the county where it is to be performed, unless there is a special contract in writing to the contrary. The contract was entered into in Riverside County and there is no evidence of a special contract in writing to the contrary. Defendant resides in Riverside County. Therefore, venue is proper in Riverside County. Plaintiffs are ordered to pay sanctions in the amount of $2,375 to defendant per CCP §396b(b). Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/LCN)

rulings.Case Title: NICOLE WILSON et al VS. OE SERVICES, LLC DBA OPEN ENGLISH LLC, A FLORIDA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Thursday, October 10, 2013, Line 11, DEFENDANT NICOLETTE MORENO'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT Defendants' demurrer is sustained in part and overruled in part. Defendants' demurrer to the second, third, fourth, and fifth causes of action is overruled. Plaintiffs plead sufficient facts to establish causes of action for violations of Labor Code §970, intentional and negligent misrepresentation, and breach of contract. Plaintiffs allege sufficient facts with particularity as to the "who, what, when, to whom, and by means" of Defendants' alleged misrepresentations. Plaintiffs fraud claims are actionable as Plaintiffs allege that Defendants knew the promises about a promotion to Vice President were false at the time they were made. (See Complaint 34, 42, 48; Lazar v. Superior Court, 12 Cal.4th 631, 638-39 (1996).) Defendants' demurrer to the sixth cause of action is overruled as to Defendant OE Services, LLC, as Plaintiffs allege sufficient facts to establish standing under the Unfair Competition Law. (Complaint, 25, 60-63.) Defendants' demurrer to the sixth cause of action regarding Defendant Moreno is sustained without leave to amend as Plaintiffs concede the claim is not meritorious. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: FRESCO MUSIC, LLC et al VS. P&T WEST HOLDINGS, LLC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Thursday, October 10, 2013, Line 14, DEFENDANT FIDELITY NATIONAL TITLE COMPANY'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT Defendant Fidelity National Title Company's Demurrer to the Complaint is sustained without leave to amend. An escrow holder's obligations are limited to compliance with the parties' instructions. (Summit Financial Holdings, Ltd. V. Continental Lawyers Title Co. (2002) 27 Cal.4th 705, 711.) An escrow holder owes neither a fiduciary duty nor a tort duty to a non-party. (Id.) Plaintiffs have pled they were not parties to the sale. (Complaint, 31.) Therefore, Plaintiffs cannot state a negligence cause of action against Defendant Fidelity as a matter of law. No opposition filed. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/LCN)

rulings.Case Title: MICHAEL YOUNESSIAN VS. PAUL NOUNOU et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Requiring Pltf To Post Security Under Ccp 391 For Stya Of Proceeding And For Order Requiring Pltf To Obtain A Pre-Filing Order;
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Thursday, October 10, 2013, Line 15, DEFENDANT PAUL NOUNOU'S Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Requiring Plaintiff To Post Security Under CCP 391 For Stay Of Proceeding And For Order Requiring Plaintiff To Obtain A Pre-Filing Order Continued to October 29, 2013 to give opposing party the opportunity to comply with Local Rule 2.6B. Opposing party must deliver courtesy copies of the opposition papers to Department 302, with a cover letter stating the new hearing date, no later than October 10, 2013 at 4:00 pm or the motion will be granted. Any exhibit must be separated by an exhibit tab per CRC 3.1110(f). The court has courtesy copies of the moving papers and reply. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. =(302/LCN)
